mechanism_narrative: Accommodative monetary policy indicated by a low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of lowest-quartile FEDFUNDS observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate crossing below 6.0% signals labor market slack exhaustion that accelerates wage-driven consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 6.0% coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months during any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Coordinated acceleration in retail sales and industrial production reflects a demand-supply feedback loop where consumption growth validates production increases, amplifying earnings optimism and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO month-over-month changes produces a negative rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns (rank IC < 0) significant at the 5% level over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During rising inflation regimes, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index reduce real corporate earnings expectations and heighten monetary tightening fears, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Months with positive CPIAUCSL surprises in high-inflation regimes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production serves as a high-frequency proxy for aggregate economic momentum; positive surprises directly lead to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 short-term returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 600 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods and construction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST below 600k predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll employment growth and a low unemployment rate capture late-cycle labor market tightness that squeezes corporate profit margins through accelerating wage costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS growing and UNRATE below 5.0% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is positive and significant (p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate rising above 2.5% restricts credit availability and increases debt-service burdens for leveraged firms, triggering a repricing of risk assets and negative S&P 500 short-term returns.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS above 2.5% coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over any 3-year rolling window.
